notes: Package can be consumed as a validated spec pack for JIT materialization.
Runtime strategy remains specification-based until a retained implementation is separately promoted.
Rollback is to disable or demote the package and route digital subscription operations work to the orchestrator or human review.

required_task_metadata: work type: incident, access-readiness, change-readiness, integration-risk, exception, client delivery, or completion evidence
tenant, business unit, product, client, subscriber segment, platform, environment, channel, jurisdiction, engagement scope, and content package
affected systems, interfaces, owners, approvals, systems of record, evidence artifacts, lifecycle state, and target output shape
runtime_assumptions_to_retrieve: tenant policies, operating constraints, escalation thresholds, quality standards, confidentiality rules, approval matrix, subscriber communication rules, and rollback expectations
current systems of record, record locations, log retention, retrieval hooks, access owner, change owner, incident owner, billing owner, product owner, and client delivery owner
client commitments, contract boundaries, approved recipients, approved delivery channels, data-use constraints, source-of-truth hierarchy, and content-rights constraints
regulated_lane_caveats: Subscription operations may identify cancellation, negative-option, renewal-notice, refund, payment, privacy, editorial, security, or contractual triggers but must not interpret or approve them.
Access and change readiness support does not authorize access grants, security exceptions, production code changes, payment processor overrides, or engineering release approval.
Subscriber data, payment metadata, entitlement logs, content-use records, platform credentials, and client communications require tenant-approved confidentiality handling.
orchestrator_return_conditions: required tenant facts, records, approvals, source context, or retrieval hooks are missing and would change the answer
public sources, tenant policy, platform policy, contract terms, security controls, privacy obligations, payment rules, consumer-rights obligations, or client instructions materially conflict
the request requires legal, editorial, security, engineering, privacy, financial, billing, contractual, executive, or final client authority

notes: Structured evidence supports the issue #1472 validated claim.
Lifecycle state transitions are evidence-gated and reversible when evidence fails.
No retained implementation is claimed by this package.
